Sent by Theo at 21:20 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 1520 customers in the E3 2 area by diverting power around an underground network fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the E3 2 area and are investigating the fault. You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to 845 customers. There are now 675 properties still affected and using an average time frame we're estimating all power will be on between 21:30 and 22:30, but this may change later depending on what our staff find. We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Theo at 20:59.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. Steps.Message: Sent by Simon at 09:16 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 09:05. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 08:59 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are currently checking our network equipment throughout the area. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now arrived on site in the Lewes Road RH17 area to investigate the cause of the voltage issues.

Our estimated time frame of 11:00-12:00 hasn't changed, but our staff will be checking our network equipment and cabling throughout the area to identify any problems. Depending on what they find, it's possible our time frame may need to change later.

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. If you have any concerns, our team are always here on 08003163105. Sent by Simon at 08:49.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 08:28- Engineers are on their way to investigate the cause of the voltage issues in the Lewes Road RH17 area.

We appreciate that power flickering and lights dimming can be concerning. Usually, this is due to interference with cabling and can sometimes cause network safety equipment to partially operate, meaning not all power is coming through to your property as normal.

At the moment our time frame to have power back on is between 11:00 and 12:00, but once our staff have fully assessed the situation this could change. Also in some cases we have to restore power in stages; so it's normal to continue receiving messages for a while even after your power has come back on.

We'll be in touch again when we arrive on site.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team
